claim: Direct measurement of all six sub-metrics (M1, M2 L1, M2 L2, M3, M4, M5) on the UD_Basque-BDT corpus will validate (or invalidate) the published reference ranges used in H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-EXPANSION-01. If Hand A's locked metric values fall within the directly-measured Basque corpus ranges, the Basque structural match is corpus-confirmed and the PROVISIONAL_CONFIRMATION from H-BV-BASQUE-MATCH-VALIDATION-01 upgrades. If direct measurements diverge from the published ranges, the original comparison used miscalibrated references and Hand A's fit must be re-evaluated against the corrected Basque values.
evidence_for: H-BV-BASQUE-MATCH-VALIDATION-01 S4 already computed M1 directly on UD_Basque-BDT (measured = 3.38, in published [2.5, 4.5]) and M3 directly (measured = 5.53, slightly above published upper bound 5.0). Two data points suggest the published ranges are mostly accurate but not tight — worth extending to M2/M4/M5.

Corpus-direct measurement is the strongest possible corroboration for a structural-match claim. Every prior Basque comparison has been against typological description ranges (Hualde, de Rijk) rather than against reproducible corpus statistics. This hypothesis closes that methodological gap.

Methodology lesson 3 (wiki/methodology-lessons.md): external baselines must be reproducible on a reference corpus before being applied to new data. Brain-V cited Basque published ranges without corpus-direct validation; this hypothesis retroactively applies the discipline.

UD_Basque-BDT is a public treebank (39,456 content words from a 5.16 MB file Brain-V has already downloaded). Every metric Brain-V wants to measure can be computed from the CoNLL-U annotations. No external resource dependency beyond what was already obtained.
evidence_against: Brain-V's M2 L1 / M2 L2 definitions are positional (glyph-unit index in tokenised word). UD_Basque's morphological annotations are category-label-based (Case, Number, Mood features), not positional. The operational mapping from 'position N-2 glyph inventory' to 'morphemes at inner slot' is approximate and interpretive. A strict correspondence requires knowing how Basque morphemes surface in glyph sequences, which is exactly the question Brain-V is trying to answer.

Brain-V's M4 gap density is computed over a (top-10 inner × top-5 outer) cross-tabulation of glyph positions. UD_Basque's equivalent would be a (morpheme-category-1 × morpheme-category-2) cross-tabulation. The cell-count scales differ (50 vs potentially hundreds of cells), so gap-density ratios are not directly comparable without normalisation.

M5 slot ordering strictness depends on which Layer-1 sub-classes are chosen. Brain-V splits vowel vs consonant inner glyphs; a Basque corpus equivalent would split some morphological category. The decision of which split to use is interpretive, and the measured M5 depends on this choice.

If Hand A's values end up inside the directly-measured UD_Basque ranges, that's meaningful confirmation. But it would show 'Hand A is consistent with an emergent representation of Basque-like morphological structure' rather than 'Hand A encodes Basque' — the latter requires decipherment-level evidence beyond structural profile.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  DATA: UD_Basque-BDT train file at raw/corpus/reference-corpora/eu_bdt-ud-train.conllu (already downloaded 5.16 MB, 39,456 content words).

  HAND A LOCKED VALUES (from H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-SCREEN-01):
    M1=4.12, M2 L1=10, M2 L2=5, M3=3.16, M4=0.20, M5=1.00

  PUBLISHED RANGES USED IN EXPANSION-01:
    M1 [2.5, 4.5]  M2 L1 [8, 16]  M2 L2 [3, 8]  M3 [2.0, 5.0]  M4 [0.02, 0.20]  M5 [0.60, 1.00]

  OPERATIONAL MAPPING (LOCKED): Brain-V's glyph-positional metrics are mapped to UD_Basque morpheme-category measurements as follows:

    M1_direct: mean count of morphological feature values per content word.
      Content word = POS in {NOUN, VERB, ADJ, PROPN}.
      Morpheme count = 1 (for the lemma) + count of non-empty feature=value pairs in the FEATS field.
      Already computed in H-BV-BASQUE-MATCH-VALIDATION-01 S4: 3.38 mean.

    M2 L1_direct: count of distinct morpheme VALUES appearing in INNER-POSITION features. Locked inner categories = {Number, Aspect, Person, PronType}. For each category, count distinct values observed in the corpus; sum across categories.
      Rationale: these categories typically mark the closest-to-stem inflection in Basque nominal/verbal templates.
      Compute: L1_direct = sum over C in {Number, Aspect, Person, PronType} of |distinct values of C observed|.

    M2 L2_direct: count of distinct morpheme VALUES appearing in OUTER-POSITION features. Locked outer categories = {Case, Mood, Tense, Definite, VerbForm}.
      Rationale: these categories typically mark the outermost inflection in Basque nominal/verbal templates.
      Compute: L2_direct = sum over C in {Case, Mood, Tense, Definite, VerbForm} of |distinct values of C observed|.

    M3_direct: mean number of distinct inflected surface forms per productive lemma (lemma appearing >= 3 times as a content word).
      Already computed in H-BV-BASQUE-MATCH-VALIDATION-01 S4: 5.53 mean, 1796 productive lemmas.

    M4_direct: gap density in the (Number × Case) cross-tabulation.
      For each pair (number_value, case_value) observed anywhere in the corpus, check whether that exact pair occurs on at least one content word.
      Cell_count = count of tokens with that specific (number_value, case_value).
      Gap threshold: cell_count == 0 AND at least one token with that number value AND at least one token with that case value.
      M4_direct = zero_cells / total_cells_with_both_axes_nonzero

    M5_direct: slot ordering strictness of Case values across Number values.
      For each Case value c, compute proportion of tokens with c that have Number=Sing vs Number=Plur.
      If ratio (sing/plur or plur/sing) >= 3.0, the Case shows >= 3x asymmetry.
      M5_direct = fraction of Case values with >=3x Number asymmetry AND at least 20 tokens total.

  COMPARISON PROCEDURE:
    For each sub-metric i:
      PUBLISHED_VALIDATED_i iff i_direct is inside the published range used in EXPANSION-01
      HAND_A_FITS_i iff Hand A's value is inside the directly-measured Basque value (for single-value metrics M2/M4/M5) OR inside a constructed range (for distribution metrics M1/M3 — use the corpus mean ±50% as the constructed range for consistency)

    Specifically:
      M1 published range [2.5, 4.5]; M1_direct = 3.38 (already known). Published validated iff 3.38 in [2.5, 4.5] = TRUE. Hand A fits iff 4.12 in [3.38*0.5, 3.38*1.5] = [1.69, 5.07] = TRUE.
      M2 L1 published range [8, 16]; L1_direct to be measured. Published validated iff L1_direct in [8, 16]. Hand A fits iff 10 is within [L1_direct * 0.7, L1_direct * 1.3] (30% tolerance around direct value).
      M2 L2 published range [3, 8]; analogous.
      M3 published range [2.0, 5.0]; M3_direct = 5.53 (already known). Published validated iff 5.53 in [2.0, 5.0] = FALSE (measured is above upper bound). Hand A fits iff 3.16 in [5.53*0.5, 5.53*1.5] = [2.77, 8.30] = TRUE.
      M4 published range [0.02, 0.20]; M4_direct to be measured. Published validated iff M4_direct in [0.02, 0.20]. Hand A fits iff 0.20 in [M4_direct * 0.5, M4_direct * 1.5].
      M5 published range [0.60, 1.00]; M5_direct to be measured. Published validated iff M5_direct in [0.60, 1.00]. Hand A fits iff 1.00 in [max(0, M5_direct - 0.1), min(1.0, M5_direct + 0.1)] (absolute ±0.1 band for a proportion metric).

  DECISION RULE (LOCKED):
    CORPUS_CONFIRMED: Hand A fits all 6 sub-metrics (6/6). Upgrade PROVISIONAL to CONFIRMED; v2 can drop the 'approximate reference ranges' caveat.
    CORPUS_PARTIAL: Hand A fits 4-5 sub-metrics with small deviations on remaining. Specify which metrics deviate.
    CORPUS_WEAK: Hand A fits 3 sub-metrics. The PROVISIONAL_CONFIRMATION was too strong. Reframe as 'Basque-like with several quantitative divergences'.
    CORPUS_REFUTED: Hand A fits 2 or fewer. The Basque structural match does not survive direct-corpus measurement; v2 cannot carry the Basque-specific claim forward.
    PUBLISHED_RANGE_FAILURE: at least 3 of 6 published ranges do not include the direct measurement. If triggered, flag that the EXPANSION-01 reference ranges were substantially miscalibrated and the re-evaluation against corrected values is the primary finding (not Hand A's fit per se).

  WORKED EXAMPLE (M2 L1 direct computation):
    Scan all NOUN, VERB, ADJ, PROPN tokens in UD_Basque-BDT. For each, parse FEATS.
    For each token with Number=Sing, add 'Sing' to the set of Number values observed. For each with Number=Plur, add 'Plur'.
    Repeat for Aspect (values like Perf, Imp, Prog...), Person (1, 2, 3), PronType (Prs, Dem, Ind...).
    L1_direct = |Number_values| + |Aspect_values| + |Person_values| + |PronType_values|
    Compare to published [8, 16]. Compare to Hand A's 10 using the 30% tolerance band.
test_metric: 6 sub-metrics directly measured on UD_Basque-BDT; each with PUBLISHED_VALIDATED (range contains direct) and HAND_A_FITS (Hand A inside tolerance around direct) boolean outcomes.
test_threshold: CORPUS_CONFIRMED if Hand A fits 6/6. CORPUS_PARTIAL if 4-5/6. CORPUS_WEAK if 3/6. CORPUS_REFUTED if <=2/6. PUBLISHED_RANGE_FAILURE overlay if >=3/6 published ranges miss their direct measurements.
tests_remaining: locked_direct_corpus_basque_test: parse UD_Basque-BDT; compute M1-M5 direct values; compare each to published range and to Hand A; classify verdict
reasoning: H-BV-BASQUE-MATCH-VALIDATION-01 upgraded H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-EXPANSION-01's result to PROVISIONAL_CONFIRMATION. Two of the four stress dimensions (S1 provenance, S2 sensitivity) came back PARTIAL. S4 corpus started direct UD_Basque measurement on M1 and M3 only. This hypothesis extends the corpus-direct measurement to the remaining four sub-metrics (M2 L1, M2 L2, M4, M5), closing the methodology gap left by the prior test. The operational mapping between Brain-V's glyph-positional metrics and UD_Basque's morpheme-category annotations is locked in this pre-reg — it is interpretive but transparent. If Hand A's locked values fall within the directly-measured Basque ranges, the PROVISIONAL qualifier drops and v2 can cite the Basque structural match as corpus-confirmed. If they don't, the PROVISIONAL was still too strong and the finding reframes to honest weakness.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-17 user-directed direct-corpus extension of H-BV-BASQUE-MATCH-VALIDATION-01. Builds on UD_Basque-BDT corpus already fetched in S4 of the parent hypothesis.
